page_content:
Prerequisite: PSYC100, BSCI170 and BSCI171; or equivalent.

Credit Only Granted for. PSYC301 or PSYC304.

Formerly: PSYC301.

PSYC307 Collective Behavior and Decision Making in Human and Animal Groups (3 Credits)

Overview of the fundamental principles underlying the organization of animal societies, mathematical models describing these phenomena, and applications to human societies and decision-making. Students

will engage in discussions of case studies, analyze and evaluate mathematical simulations, and apply what they have learned from animal groups to practice solving problems currently facing human society. Prerequisite: NEUR200, PSYC304 or PSYC221.

Recommended: PSYC200 or equivalent statistics course.

Credit Only Granted for. PSYC207 or PSYC307.
